visual_checks: Check if any audio plays automatically when the page loads
Look for auto-playing video elements with sound enabled
Check for background music or ambient audio that starts on page load
Look for a visible mechanism to pause, stop, or mute the audio within the first few elements of the page
Check if auto-playing audio has a volume control available
Look for audio advertisements or promotional content that auto-plays
pass_conditions: No audio plays automatically on page load
OR audio that auto-plays lasts less than 3 seconds
OR a mechanism to pause or stop the audio or control its volume independently from the system volume is provided at the top of the page
The audio control mechanism is easy to find and is keyboard-accessible
Mute/pause control is encountered before or near the beginning of the page content
fail_conditions: Audio plays automatically for more than 3 seconds with no mechanism to stop, pause, or mute it
Auto-playing audio has a pause control but it is buried deep in the page, not near the top
The audio control requires mouse interaction and is not keyboard-accessible
Background audio loops continuously with no way to stop it
Audio auto-plays and the only way to stop it is to mute the entire system volume
na_conditions: No audio content auto-plays on the page
All audio requires user-initiated play action (click/tap to play)
Page contains no audio or video elements
Every <video autoplay> element on the page has the muted attribute (muted autoplay plays no audio and does NOT trigger this criterion)
The [AUTOPLAY MEDIA PROBE -- DETERMINISTIC] block in the user prompt reports has_autoplay_audio=false
ne_conditions: AI response could not be parsed
Cannot verify auto-play behavior from static screenshots alone
Page behavior differs between initial load and subsequent visits
common_mistakes: Background music that auto-plays on a homepage for 'ambiance' with a tiny mute button in the corner
Video hero banners that auto-play with sound on page load
Chat widgets that play notification sounds automatically
Carousel or slider with auto-playing video advertisements
Audio control that is only visible after scrolling past the auto-playing content
auditor_anti_patterns: FALSE POSITIVE: flagging '<video autoplay muted>' (any <video> with the muted attribute set, whether autoplay is also set or not) as a WCAG 1.4.2 violation. WCAG 1.4.2 applies ONLY to content that plays AUDIO automatically. A muted video plays no audio and is compliant by definition. ALWAYS check for the muted attribute before reporting any video-autoplay finding.

GROUND TRUTH RULE: the user prompt may contain an [AUTOPLAY MEDIA PROBE -- DETERMINISTIC] block listing every <audio>, <video>, and embedded player on the page with their runtime state (autoplay, muted, duration, paused, controls). TREAT IT AS AUTHORITATIVE. If the probe reports has_autoplay_audio=false, the correct verdict is Not Applicable or Supports unless you have OTHER evidence of audio playing. Do NOT second-guess the probe by reading the HTML attributes yourself.

examples.pass: A news site has a video on the homepage that auto-plays but is muted by default — users must click the unmute button to hear audio
examples.fail: A restaurant website auto-plays background music on load that loops indefinitely, with the mute button only visible after scrolling to the footer
examples.partial: A promotional video auto-plays with sound but has a visible mute button near the video — however, the mute button is not keyboard-accessible

plain_meaning: If audio starts playing automatically when a page loads and continues for more than three seconds, the user must be able to pause it, stop it, or control its volume independently of the system volume. Auto-playing audio interferes with screen readers because both speak at once. The user must not be forced to turn down the whole machine to silence the page.
scope_applies_to: Background music or ambient audio that starts on page load
Auto-playing video with an audible track longer than 3 seconds
Audio advertisements that begin without user initiation
Embedded media players that begin playback without user action
Audio from animations, hero videos, or splash content that runs automatically
Podcast or audio widgets configured to autoplay
scope_does_not_apply_to: Audio or video that only plays after explicit user activation (click, tap, key press)
Audio lasting 3 seconds or less total
Media controls that are present and reachable (pause/stop/volume provided in the player)
Content muted by default even if playback starts automatically (no audio output until user unmutes)
Captions display, transcripts, or audio description features (those belong to SC 1.2.x)
General media accessibility like sign language or extended descriptions (SC 1.2.x)
borderline_calibration: Muted autoplay video is NOT a failure of 1.4.2 because no audio plays.
If a visible, operable pause/stop control is present at the moment audio begins, the criterion is MET regardless of duration.
Audio under 3 seconds is exempt even with no control.
A control that requires scrolling or opening a menu to reach still counts as available if it is operable by keyboard and assistive tech.
When in doubt, prefer not flagging if the audio is clearly user-initiated (click-to-play).
